prompt.content:
---
module: schemas_lock
deps: [extensions]
targets: [rust]
tests:
  - name: backend defaults to claude-cli when absent
    given: { json: "{\"model\":\"claude-sonnet-5\"}" }
    expect: { backend: claude-cli }
  - name: an explicit anthropic-api backend is kept
    given: { json: "{\"model\":\"claude-sonnet-5\",\"backend\":\"anthropic-api\"}" }
    expect: { backend: anthropic-api }
  - name: an explicit claude-cli backend is kept
    given: { json: "{\"model\":\"claude-opus-5\",\"backend\":\"claude-cli\"}" }
    expect: { backend: claude-cli, model: claude-opus-5 }
  - name: an unknown backend is rejected
    given: { json: "{\"model\":\"x\",\"backend\":\"openai\"}" }
    expect: { accepts: false }
  - name: corrupt json is rejected
    given: { json: "{not json" }
    expect: { accepts: false }
  - name: an empty model is rejected
    given: { json: "{\"model\":\"\"}" }
    expect: { accepts: false }
  - name: prompt aliases default to the curated list when absent
    given: { json: "{\"model\":\"m\"}" }
    expect: { promptAliases: curated-default }
  - name: a valid prompt-alias override is kept verbatim
    given: { json: "{\"model\":\"m\",\"promptAliases\":[\".🧑\",\".🤠\"]}" }
    expect: { promptAliases: [".🧑", ".🤠"] }
  - name: an alias without a leading dot is rejected
    given: { json: "{\"model\":\"m\",\"promptAliases\":[\"🧑\"]}" }
    expect: { accepts: false }
  - name: an alias with more than two code points after the dot is rejected
    given: { json: "{\"model\":\"m\",\"promptAliases\":[\".abc\"]}" }
    expect: { accepts: false }
  - name: an alias containing a ZWJ sequence is rejected
    given: { json: "{\"model\":\"m\",\"promptAliases\":[\".👨‍💻\"]}" }
    expect: { accepts: false }
  - name: the agent config defaults to the claude preset when absent
    given: { json: "{\"model\":\"m\"}" }
    expect: { agentPreset: claude }
  - name: the codex preset is accepted
    given: { json: "{\"model\":\"m\",\"agent\":{\"preset\":\"codex\"}}" }
    expect: { agentPreset: codex }
  - name: the custom preset with a command is accepted
    given: { json: "{\"model\":\"m\",\"agent\":{\"preset\":\"custom\",\"command\":[\"mycli\",\"--task-file\",\"{task}\"]}}" }
    expect: { agentPreset: custom, command: ["mycli", "--task-file", "{task}"] }
  - name: an unknown preset is rejected
    given: { json: "{\"model\":\"m\",\"agent\":{\"preset\":\"gpt\"}}" }
    expect: { accepts: false }
  - name: the custom preset without a command is rejected
    given: { json: "{\"model\":\"m\",\"agent\":{\"preset\":\"custom\"}}" }
    expect: { accepts: false }
  - name: the custom preset with an empty command is rejected
    given: { json: "{\"model\":\"m\",\"agent\":{\"preset\":\"custom\",\"command\":[]}}" }
    expect: { accepts: false }
  - name: the claude preset with a command is rejected
    given: { json: "{\"model\":\"m\",\"agent\":{\"preset\":\"claude\",\"command\":[\"x\"]}}" }
    expect: { accepts: false }
  - name: an unknown field inside the agent config is rejected
    given: { json: "{\"model\":\"m\",\"agent\":{\"preset\":\"claude\",\"extra\":1}}" }
    expect: { accepts: false }
---
# The lock document: model / backend / agent / prompt-alias configuration

This module defines the serde types and validators for the NAPL lock document —
the small JSON configuration that pins which model, code-generation backend,
coding-agent engine, and prompt aliases the toolchain compiles through. It is
pure: no I/O. Bring in `serde` and `serde_json`.

## Where this code lives

The working directory is a Cargo workspace whose root manifest is written and
owned by the toolchain — leave it alone. Create this module as its own member
crate in a subdirectory named `schemas_lock/`: `schemas_lock/Cargo.toml`
(package name `schemas_lock`) and `schemas_lock/src/lib.rs`. Touch nothing
outside `schemas_lock/`. Ensure `cargo test` passes from the workspace root
before finishing.

## Builds on the `extensions` module of this workspace

This module builds on the **`extensions`** module of this same workspace. The
`extensions` module is generated as a sibling member crate in the `extensions/`
directory (so `../extensions` relative to this crate). It exposes a public
function `default_prompt_aliases() -> Vec<String>` returning the curated default
list of prompt-alias strings.

Add a path dependency on that sibling member crate in your `Cargo.toml`
(depend on the `extensions` crate by path — it lives one directory up and over,
at `../extensions`) and call its `default_prompt_aliases()` for the curated
default below. Do **not** reimplement the curated alias list here, and do not
depend on any hand-written crate — depend only on the generated sibling
`extensions` member crate. This sibling path dependency is part of the same
workspace, not an external crates.io dependency, so add it even though the
general guidance is to avoid outside dependencies.

## The value types

Expose these public types, deriving the serde traits noted:

- `Backend`: an enum with two variants, `ClaudeCli` and `AnthropicApi`,
  serialized/deserialized in **kebab-case** (so the JSON spellings are
  `"claude-cli"` and `"anthropic-api"`). It derives serialize and deserialize,
  and is comparable and copyable.
- `AgentPreset`: an enum with three variants, `Claude`, `Codex`, and `Custom`,
  serialized/deserialized in **lowercase** (`"claude"`, `"codex"`, `"custom"`).
- `AgentConfig`: a struct with a public `preset: AgentPreset` and an optional
  public `command: Option<Vec<String>>` (a command template; omitted from
  serialized output and defaulting to `None` when absent). It **denies unknown
  fields** — any JSON key other than `preset` and `command` is a deserialization
  error.
- `HlLock`: the lock document itself, with a public `model: String`; a
  `backend: Backend` that defaults to claude-cli when the key is absent; an
  optional `prompt_aliases: Option<Vec<String>>` whose JSON key is
  **`promptAliases`** (camelCase), omitted when absent; and an optional
  `agent: Option<AgentConfig>` omitted when absent.

Expose these public constants and defaults:

- `DEFAULT_MODEL: &str` equal to `"claude-sonnet-5"`.
- `DEFAULT_BACKEND: Backend` equal to the claude-cli variant.
- `DEFAULT_AGENT_PRESET: AgentPreset` equal to the claude variant.
- `default_agent_config() -> AgentConfig`: the claude preset with no command.

## Parsing and validating a lock

Expose `parse_lock(raw: &str) -> Result<HlLock, _>` that deserializes a JSON
string into an `HlLock` and then validates it. Deserialization failures (corrupt
JSON, unknown backend spelling, unknown preset, unknown agent field, wrong
types) are errors, not panics. After a structural parse, enforce:

- `model` must not be empty — an empty model string is a validation error.
- If `promptAliases` is present, every alias must be valid (see below).
- If `agent` is present, it must be valid (see below).

### Prompt-alias validation

A prompt alias is a short string. Validate each one:

- It must start with a `.` (dot). An alias not beginning with `.` is rejected.
- After the leading dot it must have **1 or 2 Unicode code points** — no more,
  no fewer. So `".abc"` (three code points after the dot) is rejected.
- It must not contain a ZWJ (zero-width joiner, U+200D) anywhere. A ZWJ-joined
  emoji sequence such as `".👨‍💻"` (man + ZWJ + laptop) is rejected even though it
  is visually one glyph.

The exact error wording does not matter — only which aliases are accepted versus
rejected. A valid override list is kept verbatim.

### Agent-config validation

Validate the agent configuration by preset:

- The `custom` preset **requires** a non-empty `command` array. A custom preset
  with no `command`, or with an empty `command`, is a validation error.
- The `claude` and `codex` presets **must not** carry a `command`. Either of
  those presets with a `command` present is a validation error.

## Resolving effective configuration

Expose two resolvers:

- `resolve_prompt_aliases(lock: &HlLock) -> Vec<String>`: the lock's
  `promptAliases` override if present, otherwise the curated default obtained by
  calling the `extensions` module's `default_prompt_aliases()`.
- `resolve_agent_config(lock: &HlLock) -> AgentConfig`: the lock's `agent`
  override if present, otherwise `default_agent_config()` (the claude preset).

# The lock document: model / backend / agent / prompt-alias configuration

This module defines the serde types and validators for the NAPL lock document —
the small JSON configuration that pins which model, code-generation backend,
coding-agent engine, and prompt aliases the toolchain compiles through. It is
pure: no I/O. Bring in `serde` and `serde_json`.

## Where this code lives

The working directory is a Cargo workspace whose root manifest is written and
owned by the toolchain — leave it alone. Create this module as its own member
crate in a subdirectory named `schemas_lock/`: `schemas_lock/Cargo.toml`
(package name `schemas_lock`) and `schemas_lock/src/lib.rs`. Touch nothing
outside `schemas_lock/`. Ensure `cargo test` passes from the workspace root
before finishing.

## Builds on the `extensions` module of this workspace

This module builds on the **`extensions`** module of this same workspace. The
`extensions` module is generated as a sibling member crate in the `extensions/`
directory (so `../extensions` relative to this crate). It exposes a public
function `default_prompt_aliases() -> Vec<String>` returning the curated default
list of prompt-alias strings.

Add a path dependency on that sibling member crate in your `Cargo.toml`
(depend on the `extensions` crate by path — it lives one directory up and over,
at `../extensions`) and call its `default_prompt_aliases()` for the curated
default below. Do **not** reimplement the curated alias list here, and do not
depend on any hand-written crate — depend only on the generated sibling
`extensions` member crate. This sibling path dependency is part of the same
workspace, not an external crates.io dependency, so add it even though the
general guidance is to avoid outside dependencies.

## The value types

Expose these public types, deriving the serde traits noted:

- `Backend`: an enum with two variants, `ClaudeCli` and `AnthropicApi`,
  serialized/deserialized in **kebab-case** (so the JSON spellings are
  `"claude-cli"` and `"anthropic-api"`). It derives serialize and deserialize,
  and is comparable and copyable.
- `AgentPreset`: an enum with three variants, `Claude`, `Codex`, and `Custom`,
  serialized/deserialized in **lowercase** (`"claude"`, `"codex"`, `"custom"`).
- `AgentConfig`: a struct with a public `preset: AgentPreset` and an optional
  public `command: Option<Vec<String>>` (a command template; omitted from
  serialized output and defaulting to `None` when absent). It **denies unknown

- `HlLock`: the lock document itself, with a public `model: String`; a
  `backend: Backend` that defaults to claude-cli when the key is absent; an

  **`promptAliases`** (camelCase), omitted when absent; and an optional
  `agent: Option<AgentConfig>` omitted when absent.

Expose these public constants and defaults:

- `DEFAULT_MODEL: &str` equal to `"claude-sonnet-5"`.
- `DEFAULT_BACKEND: Backend` equal to the claude-cli variant.
- `DEFAULT_AGENT_PRESET: AgentPreset` equal to the claude variant.
- `default_agent_config() -> AgentConfig`: the claude preset with no command.

## Parsing and validating a lock

Expose `parse_lock(raw: &str) -> Result<HlLock, _>` that deserializes a JSON
string into an `HlLock` and then validates it. Deserialization failures (corrupt
JSON, unknown backend spelling, unknown preset, unknown agent field, wrong
types) are errors, not panics. After a structural parse, enforce:

- `model` must not be empty — an empty model string is a validation error.
- If `promptAliases` is present, every alias must be valid (see below).
- If `agent` is present, it must be valid (see below).

### Prompt-alias validation

A prompt alias is a short string. Validate each one:

- It must start with a `.` (dot). An alias not beginning with `.` is rejected.
- After the leading dot it must have **1 or 2 Unicode code points** — no more,
  no fewer. So `".abc"` (three code points after the dot) is rejected.
- It must not contain a ZWJ (zero-width joiner, U+200D) anywhere. A ZWJ-joined
  emoji sequence such as `".👨‍💻"` (man + ZWJ + laptop) is rejected even though it

### Agent-config validation

Validate the agent configuration by preset:

  those presets with a `command` present is a validation error.

## Resolving effective configuration

Expose two resolvers:

- `resolve_prompt_aliases(lock: &HlLock) -> Vec<String>`: the lock's

- `resolve_agent_config(lock: &HlLock) -> AgentConfig`: the lock's `agent`
  override if present, otherwise `default_agent_config()` (the claude preset).

files.content:
[package]
name = "schemas_lock"
version = "0.1.0"
edition = "2021"

[dependencies]
serde = { version = "1", features = ["derive"] }
serde_json = "1"
extensions = { path = "../extensions" }

attribution.note: command skipped when None, defaults None
backend defaults to claude-cli when absent
promptAliases camelCase key, omitted when absent
parse_lock deserializes JSON, error not panic
validate each prompt alias if present

mapl.message: NO CHANGES — existing schemas_lock code already satisfies whole prompt per agent.
mapl.reasoning: Prompt diff empty. Agent reports every type, constant, validator, and resolver already present and 20/20 tests pass from workspace root, so no edits produced. Cannot independently confirm the pre-existing source matches the contract, but nothing in the prompt was actionable as a change.

mapl.message: parse_lock error type written as `Result<HlLock, _>` — concrete error type left unspecified.
mapl.reasoning: The signature uses a placeholder `_` for the error type. Prompt never names an error type (custom enum, String, serde_json::Error, Box<dyn Error>, etc.). Whatever the existing code uses was accepted, but the contract does not pin it, so callers cannot rely on a stable error type.
mapl.suggestion: State the concrete error type, e.g. `parse_lock(raw: &str) -> Result<HlLock, LockError>` and define LockError, or explicitly say the error type is unconstrained.

mapl.message: AgentPreset derive set underspecified — only serialize/deserialize + case named; Clone/PartialEq/Copy unstated.
mapl.reasoning: Backend (line 41) is explicitly 'comparable and copyable', but AgentPreset only gets serde + lowercase casing. AgentConfig embeds AgentPreset and default_agent_config returns one, so extra derives (Clone/PartialEq) are likely needed. Prompt leaves which traits AgentPreset derives open.
mapl.suggestion: For AgentPreset, list the required derives explicitly (e.g. also comparable and copyable) as done for Backend.

mapl.message: AgentConfig derives not spelled out; assumed matching serde + clone semantics needed by resolvers/defaults.
mapl.reasoning: Line 36 says 'deriving the serde traits noted' but AgentConfig's clause only calls out serde behavior (skip when None, deny_unknown_fields) without naming Clone/PartialEq. resolve_agent_config returns AgentConfig by value and default_agent_config constructs one, implying Clone is expected. The exact derive set was left to the implementer.
mapl.suggestion: Enumerate AgentConfig's required derives (Serialize, Deserialize, Clone, and any comparison traits) explicitly.

target: rust
entries:

    kind: no-op
    message: NO CHANGES — existing schemas_lock code already satisfies whole prompt per agent.
    reasoning: Prompt diff empty. Agent reports every type, constant, validator, and resolver already present and 20/20 tests pass from workspace root, so no edits produced. Cannot independently confirm the pre-existing source matches the contract, but nothing in the prompt was actionable as a change.
  - promptLines:
      - 64
      - 64
    kind: ambiguity
    message: parse_lock error type written as `Result<HlLock, _>` — concrete error type left unspecified.
    reasoning: The signature uses a placeholder `_` for the error type. Prompt never names an error type (custom enum, String, serde_json::Error, Box<dyn Error>, etc.). Whatever the existing code uses was accepted, but the contract does not pin it, so callers cannot rely on a stable error type.
    suggestion: "State the concrete error type, e.g. `parse_lock(raw: &str) -> Result<HlLock, LockError>` and define LockError, or explicitly say the error type is unconstrained."
  - promptLines:
      - 42
      - 43
    kind: ambiguity
    message: AgentPreset derive set underspecified — only serialize/deserialize + case named; Clone/PartialEq/Copy unstated.
    reasoning: Backend (line 41) is explicitly 'comparable and copyable', but AgentPreset only gets serde + lowercase casing. AgentConfig embeds AgentPreset and default_agent_config returns one, so extra derives (Clone/PartialEq) are likely needed. Prompt leaves which traits AgentPreset derives open.
    suggestion: For AgentPreset, list the required derives explicitly (e.g. also comparable and copyable) as done for Backend.
  - promptLines:
      - 44
      - 48
    kind: assumption
    message: AgentConfig derives not spelled out; assumed matching serde + clone semantics needed by resolvers/defaults.
    reasoning: Line 36 says 'deriving the serde traits noted' but AgentConfig's clause only calls out serde behavior (skip when None, deny_unknown_fields) without naming Clone/PartialEq. resolve_agent_config returns AgentConfig by value and default_agent_config constructs one, implying Clone is expected. The exact derive set was left to the implementer.
    suggestion: Enumerate AgentConfig's required derives (Serialize, Deserialize, Clone, and any comparison traits) explicitly.

files.schemas_lock.napl:
---
module: schemas_lock
deps: [extensions]
targets: [rust]
tests:
  - name: backend defaults to claude-cli when absent
    given: { json: "{\"model\":\"claude-sonnet-5\"}" }
    expect: { backend: claude-cli }
  - name: an explicit anthropic-api backend is kept
    given: { json: "{\"model\":\"claude-sonnet-5\",\"backend\":\"anthropic-api\"}" }
    expect: { backend: anthropic-api }
  - name: an explicit claude-cli backend is kept
    given: { json: "{\"model\":\"claude-opus-5\",\"backend\":\"claude-cli\"}" }
    expect: { backend: claude-cli, model: claude-opus-5 }
  - name: an unknown backend is rejected
    given: { json: "{\"model\":\"x\",\"backend\":\"openai\"}" }
    expect: { accepts: false }
  - name: corrupt json is rejected
    given: { json: "{not json" }
    expect: { accepts: false }
  - name: an empty model is rejected
    given: { json: "{\"model\":\"\"}" }
    expect: { accepts: false }
  - name: prompt aliases default to the curated list when absent
    given: { json: "{\"model\":\"m\"}" }
    expect: { promptAliases: curated-default }
  - name: a valid prompt-alias override is kept verbatim
    given: { json: "{\"model\":\"m\",\"promptAliases\":[\".🧑\",\".🤠\"]}" }
    expect: { promptAliases: [".🧑", ".🤠"] }
  - name: an alias without a leading dot is rejected
    given: { json: "{\"model\":\"m\",\"promptAliases\":[\"🧑\"]}" }
    expect: { accepts: false }
  - name: an alias with more than two code points after the dot is rejected
    given: { json: "{\"model\":\"m\",\"promptAliases\":[\".abc\"]}" }
    expect: { accepts: false }
  - name: an alias containing a ZWJ sequence is rejected
    given: { json: "{\"model\":\"m\",\"promptAliases\":[\".👨‍💻\"]}" }
    expect: { accepts: false }
  - name: the agent config defaults to the claude preset when absent
    given: { json: "{\"model\":\"m\"}" }
    expect: { agentPreset: claude }
  - name: the codex preset is accepted
    given: { json: "{\"model\":\"m\",\"agent\":{\"preset\":\"codex\"}}" }
    expect: { agentPreset: codex }
  - name: the custom preset with a command is accepted
    given: { json: "{\"model\":\"m\",\"agent\":{\"preset\":\"custom\",\"command\":[\"mycli\",\"--task-file\",\"{task}\"]}}" }
    expect: { agentPreset: custom, command: ["mycli", "--task-file", "{task}"] }
  - name: an unknown preset is rejected
    given: { json: "{\"model\":\"m\",\"agent\":{\"preset\":\"gpt\"}}" }
    expect: { accepts: false }
  - name: the custom preset without a command is rejected
    given: { json: "{\"model\":\"m\",\"agent\":{\"preset\":\"custom\"}}" }
    expect: { accepts: false }
  - name: the custom preset with an empty command is rejected
    given: { json: "{\"model\":\"m\",\"agent\":{\"preset\":\"custom\",\"command\":[]}}" }
    expect: { accepts: false }
  - name: the claude preset with a command is rejected
    given: { json: "{\"model\":\"m\",\"agent\":{\"preset\":\"claude\",\"command\":[\"x\"]}}" }
    expect: { accepts: false }
  - name: an unknown field inside the agent config is rejected
    given: { json: "{\"model\":\"m\",\"agent\":{\"preset\":\"claude\",\"extra\":1}}" }
    expect: { accepts: false }
---
# The lock document: model / backend / agent / prompt-alias configuration

This module defines the serde types and validators for the NAPL lock document —
the small JSON configuration that pins which model, code-generation backend,
coding-agent engine, and prompt aliases the toolchain compiles through. It is
pure: no I/O. Bring in `serde` and `serde_json`.

## Where this code lives

The working directory is a Cargo workspace whose root manifest is written and
owned by the toolchain — leave it alone. Create this module as its own member
crate in a subdirectory named `schemas_lock/`: `schemas_lock/Cargo.toml`
(package name `schemas_lock`) and `schemas_lock/src/lib.rs`. Touch nothing
outside `schemas_lock/`. Ensure `cargo test` passes from the workspace root
before finishing.

## Builds on the `extensions` module of this workspace

This module builds on the **`extensions`** module of this same workspace. The
`extensions` module is generated as a sibling member crate in the `extensions/`
directory (so `../extensions` relative to this crate). It exposes a public
function `default_prompt_aliases() -> Vec<String>` returning the curated default
list of prompt-alias strings.

Add a path dependency on that sibling member crate in your `Cargo.toml`
(depend on the `extensions` crate by path — it lives one directory up and over,
at `../extensions`) and call its `default_prompt_aliases()` for the curated
default below. Do **not** reimplement the curated alias list here, and do not
depend on any hand-written crate — depend only on the generated sibling
`extensions` member crate. This sibling path dependency is part of the same
workspace, not an external crates.io dependency, so add it even though the
general guidance is to avoid outside dependencies.

## The value types

Expose these public types, deriving the serde traits noted:

- `Backend`: an enum with two variants, `ClaudeCli` and `AnthropicApi`,
  serialized/deserialized in **kebab-case** (so the JSON spellings are
  `"claude-cli"` and `"anthropic-api"`). It derives serialize and deserialize,
  and is comparable and copyable.
- `AgentPreset`: an enum with three variants, `Claude`, `Codex`, and `Custom`,
  serialized/deserialized in **lowercase** (`"claude"`, `"codex"`, `"custom"`).
- `AgentConfig`: a struct with a public `preset: AgentPreset` and an optional
  public `command: Option<Vec<String>>` (a command template; omitted from
  serialized output and defaulting to `None` when absent). It **denies unknown
  fields** — any JSON key other than `preset` and `command` is a deserialization
  error.
- `HlLock`: the lock document itself, with a public `model: String`; a
  `backend: Backend` that defaults to claude-cli when the key is absent; an
  optional `prompt_aliases: Option<Vec<String>>` whose JSON key is
  **`promptAliases`** (camelCase), omitted when absent; and an optional
  `agent: Option<AgentConfig>` omitted when absent.

Expose these public constants and defaults:

- `DEFAULT_MODEL: &str` equal to `"claude-sonnet-5"`.
- `DEFAULT_BACKEND: Backend` equal to the claude-cli variant.
- `DEFAULT_AGENT_PRESET: AgentPreset` equal to the claude variant.
- `default_agent_config() -> AgentConfig`: the claude preset with no command.

## Parsing and validating a lock

Expose `parse_lock(raw: &str) -> Result<HlLock, _>` that deserializes a JSON
string into an `HlLock` and then validates it. Deserialization failures (corrupt
JSON, unknown backend spelling, unknown preset, unknown agent field, wrong
types) are errors, not panics. After a structural parse, enforce:

- `model` must not be empty — an empty model string is a validation error.
- If `promptAliases` is present, every alias must be valid (see below).
- If `agent` is present, it must be valid (see below).

### Prompt-alias validation

A prompt alias is a short string. Validate each one:

- It must start with a `.` (dot). An alias not beginning with `.` is rejected.
- After the leading dot it must have **1 or 2 Unicode code points** — no more,
  no fewer. So `".abc"` (three code points after the dot) is rejected.
- It must not contain a ZWJ (zero-width joiner, U+200D) anywhere. A ZWJ-joined
  emoji sequence such as `".👨‍💻"` (man + ZWJ + laptop) is rejected even though it
  is visually one glyph.

The exact error wording does not matter — only which aliases are accepted versus
rejected. A valid override list is kept verbatim.

### Agent-config validation

Validate the agent configuration by preset:

- The `custom` preset **requires** a non-empty `command` array. A custom preset
  with no `command`, or with an empty `command`, is a validation error.
- The `claude` and `codex` presets **must not** carry a `command`. Either of
  those presets with a `command` present is a validation error.

## Resolving effective configuration

Expose two resolvers:

- `resolve_prompt_aliases(lock: &HlLock) -> Vec<String>`: the lock's
  `promptAliases` override if present, otherwise the curated default obtained by
  calling the `extensions` module's `default_prompt_aliases()`.
- `resolve_agent_config(lock: &HlLock) -> AgentConfig`: the lock's `agent`
  override if present, otherwise `default_agent_config()` (the claude preset).

[package]
name = "schemas_lock"
version = "0.1.0"
edition = "2021"

[dependencies]
serde = { version = "1", features = ["derive"] }
serde_json = "1"
extensions = { path = "../extensions" }

entries.note: command skipped when None, defaults None
backend defaults to claude-cli when absent
promptAliases camelCase key, omitted when absent
parse_lock deserializes JSON, error not panic
validate each prompt alias if present

entry.message: NO CHANGES — existing schemas_lock code already satisfies whole prompt per agent.
entry.reasoning: Prompt diff empty. Agent reports every type, constant, validator, and resolver already present and 20/20 tests pass from workspace root, so no edits produced. Cannot independently confirm the pre-existing source matches the contract, but nothing in the prompt was actionable as a change.

entry.message: parse_lock error type written as `Result<HlLock, _>` — concrete error type left unspecified.
entry.reasoning: The signature uses a placeholder `_` for the error type. Prompt never names an error type (custom enum, String, serde_json::Error, Box<dyn Error>, etc.). Whatever the existing code uses was accepted, but the contract does not pin it, so callers cannot rely on a stable error type.
entry.suggestion: State the concrete error type, e.g. `parse_lock(raw: &str) -> Result<HlLock, LockError>` and define LockError, or explicitly say the error type is unconstrained.

entry.message: AgentPreset derive set underspecified — only serialize/deserialize + case named; Clone/PartialEq/Copy unstated.
entry.reasoning: Backend (line 41) is explicitly 'comparable and copyable', but AgentPreset only gets serde + lowercase casing. AgentConfig embeds AgentPreset and default_agent_config returns one, so extra derives (Clone/PartialEq) are likely needed. Prompt leaves which traits AgentPreset derives open.
entry.suggestion: For AgentPreset, list the required derives explicitly (e.g. also comparable and copyable) as done for Backend.

entry.message: AgentConfig derives not spelled out; assumed matching serde + clone semantics needed by resolvers/defaults.
entry.reasoning: Line 36 says 'deriving the serde traits noted' but AgentConfig's clause only calls out serde behavior (skip when None, deny_unknown_fields) without naming Clone/PartialEq. resolve_agent_config returns AgentConfig by value and default_agent_config constructs one, implying Clone is expected. The exact derive set was left to the implementer.
entry.suggestion: Enumerate AgentConfig's required derives (Serialize, Deserialize, Clone, and any comparison traits) explicitly.
